feat: agenda system, bug fix (#123)

* feat: topic agenda system with concept-based onboarding

Replace flat skill selection with a structured topic agenda that
supports role-based concept browsing, prerequisite-aware ordering,
and configurable assessment thoroughness (quick/standard/thorough).

Backend:
- Add AgendaItem, TopicStatus, Thoroughness state types
- Add build_agenda pipeline node between calibration and assessment
- Add GET /roles/{role_id}/concepts endpoint with topo-sorted concepts
- Add display_name field to all knowledge base YAML concepts
- Infer confidence for un-assessed concepts from assessed prerequisites
- Dynamic question budget based on agenda size and thoroughness
- Conclude assessment when no pending topics remain

Frontend:
- Replace SkillBrowser with ConceptBrowser grouped by level
- Fetch concepts per role/level via new API endpoint
- Add max 10 concept selection limit with disabled state
- Show estimated question count from thoroughness setting

Mount an init script in /docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/ so the test
database is created alongside the main database on first container
startup. Prevents local test failures after volume recreation.

commit.message:
Fix movement: use frame-step walk model for wall-safe movement

Replace target-tile model with a step-based loop that consumes movement budget
each frame. Direction changes and wall checks only happen when exactly at a tile
center (integer snap point), making it physically impossible to enter a wall.

feat: add Vitest test environment and CI test job (#21)

No automated tests or test infrastructure existed; CI only ran type
checks and builds.

## Changes

- **`vitest.config.ts`** — new Vitest config with `jsdom` environment
for browser API access (`localStorage`, `Notification`)
- **`src/storage.test.ts`** — 16 unit tests covering all CRUD functions
including `notified` reset logic in `updateProduct`
- **`src/notifications.test.ts`** — 9 unit tests for
`getDaysUntilExpiry` (boundary cases) and `checkAndNotify` (permission
guard, threshold logic, deduplication via `notified` flag)
- **`package.json`** — adds `"test": "vitest run"` script
- **`tsconfig.json`** — includes `vitest.config.ts` in compilation scope
- **`.github/workflows/ci.yml`** — adds `test` job running `npm test`
alongside existing `typecheck` and `build` jobs

Module mocking uses `vi.mock` hoisted at the top level; `Notification`
global is stubbed per-test via `vi.stubGlobal`:

```ts
vi.mock('./storage.ts', () => ({
  loadProducts: vi.fn(),
  markNotified: vi.fn(),
}));

beforeEach(() => {
  const MockNotification = vi.fn();
  Object.defineProperty(MockNotification, 'permission', { value: 'granted', writable: true, configurable: true });
  vi.stubGlobal('Notification', MockNotification);
});
```

commit.message:
fix: edit modal save closes window and shows save feedback (#20)

The save button in the edit modal had no error boundary — if
`updateProduct` or `renderProductList` threw (e.g. localStorage quota
exceeded), `cleanup()` was never reached and the modal stayed open.
There was also no confirmation that the save succeeded.

## Changes

- **`showToast(message, type)`** — new helper that appends a temporary
`role="status"` / `aria-live="polite"` div at the top of the screen,
fades out after 3 s
- **`onSave` wrapped in try/catch:**
- Success → `cleanup()` closes modal, then green `✅ Produkt gespeichert`
toast
- Error → inline `edit-error-msg` shows `"Fehler beim Speichern. Bitte
versuche es erneut."`, modal stays open for retry

```ts
try {
  updateProduct(product.id, { name, expiryDate, notifyDaysBefore });
  renderProductList(container);
  checkAndNotify();
  cleanup();
  showToast('✅ Produkt gespeichert');
} catch {
  if (errorMsg) errorMsg.textContent = 'Fehler beim Speichern. Bitte versuche es erneut.';
}
```

## Screenshot

![Modal open before
save](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/14683926-a0ae-4c6a-bfa2-729bd1a79b5d)
![Modal closed with success
toast](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7f085375-e2f1-4f06-bef8-ce18b86dd2e1)

Wall checks happen before setting the next target tile, so it is impossible
for either entity to enter a wall tile.

commit.message:
Add Pac-Man game

Complete client-side Pac-Man game built with HTML5 Canvas, CSS3, and vanilla JavaScript.

Features:
- 60FPS game loop with requestAnimationFrame
- 4 ghosts with BFS pathfinding (Chase/Scatter/Frightened/Eaten modes)
- Power pellets, dot collection, lives system
- Scoring: dots=10, pellets=50, ghosts=200/400/800/1600
- Web Audio API sound effects (no external files)
- Keyboard (Arrow/WASD) + touch swipe + D-pad controls
- Mobile responsive canvas scaling
- 2 levels with increasing difficulty

feat(orchestrator): add configurable drain timeout to circuit breaker

Add circuit_breaker_drain_timeout config (default 900s/15min) that limits how
long the drain phase waits for remaining agents after the circuit breaker trips.
When timeout is exceeded, remaining agents are forcibly terminated via
_drain_orphaned_futures. Setting to 0 disables the timeout (wait forever).

Fixes: Circuit breaker drain phase had no timeout, causing stuck agents to block
graceful shutdown indefinitely (observed 70-minute stall in run 20260324_172150).

fix: include WP REST nonce in analytics track endpoint for logged-in users (#47731)

* fix: include WP REST nonce in analytics track endpoint for logged-in users

WordPress REST API resets the authenticated user when cookies are present
but no nonce is sent. The analytics sendBeacon/fetch calls send cookies
but never included a nonce, causing store_admin to always be 0.

Append _wpnonce query param to trackEndpoint URL for logged-in users so
the REST API preserves authentication context.

to catch payload shape drift between Python backend and TypeScript frontend.

- Integrate validation in useBridge.ts and useSetupBridge.ts
- Add 32 comprehensive tests covering valid/invalid payload scenarios
- Document architecture, maintenance, and best practices

clear, actionable error messages at the bridge boundary instead of deep in
rendering code.

feat(desktop): Add runtime-validated contracts for Python↔TypeScript bridge

- Install Zod validation library for runtime schema validation
- Define comprehensive Zod schemas for all bridge payloads in schemas.ts
- Add validation at bridge boundary in useBridge.ts and useSetupBridge.ts
- Use validatePayload for critical paths (fail-fast with actionable errors)
- Use safeValidatePayload for incremental updates (graceful degradation)
- Add 32 comprehensive tests covering valid/invalid payloads
- Tests verify contract mismatches surface quickly with clear error messages

This addresses the issue where Python backend and TypeScript frontend relied
on mirrored interfaces without runtime validation, making UI regressions easy
when payload shapes drifted. Now contract violations fail at the boundary
with actionable error messages instead of silently breaking the UI.

commit.message:
Fix circuit-sim for Windows: text rawfile + encoding

- parse_rawfile.py: Support both binary and text (Values:) rawfile
  formats. Windows ngspice outputs text format even with -r flag.
- run_sim.py: Add encoding='utf-8' to all NamedTemporaryFile and
  Path.read_text() calls. Fixes UnicodeEncodeError on Windows
  where the default encoding is cp1252.

Tested: all three example netlists (rc_lowpass, pulse_response,
rc_sweep) pass end-to-end on Windows with ngspice 45.2.
